headlines.title: Migrants making false domestic abuse claims to stay in UK, BBC investigation finds
headlines.description: In the third part of an undercover investigation, the BBC reveals how rules aimed at protecting abuse victims are being exploited.

headlines.title: Breakthrough £90,000 Alzheimer's drugs unlikely to benefit patients, report suggests
headlines.description: A major review has provoked a backlash after concluding the medicines provide too little benefit to be noticed.

headlines.title: UK prepares for food shortages in worst case scenario as Iran war continues
headlines.description: The UK could face some food shortages by the summer under a worst case scenario drawn up by officials.

headlines.title: Lyse Doucet in Iran: Under fragile ceasefire, Iranians wonder if US deal can be done
headlines.description: The BBC's chief international correspondent reports from Iran as diplomatic efforts to avoid a return to war intensify.

headlines.title: Why cheap power could matter more than clean power in the push for net zero
headlines.description: The question of how important making our electricity clean is to going green is coming under increasing scrutiny

headlines.title: 'I was the most trolled person in the world,' Meghan says during Australia visit
headlines.description: Alongside her husband, the Duchess of Sussex was speaking to young people in Melbourne about the harms of social media.

headlines.title: Economy grew faster than expected ahead of Iran war
headlines.description: The economy saw its biggest monthly rise in more than two years just before the outbreak of the US-Israeli war with Iran.

headlines.title: The city where primary school places come with a toilet-training guide
headlines.description: Teachers say more children are starting school without skills like basic communication and potty training.

headlines.title: Wave of Russian drone and missile attacks kill at least 15 in Ukraine
headlines.description: Meanwhile, a Ukrainian drone attack killed two children in Russia, officials say.

headlines.title: Nine universities start legal action over student loan error row
headlines.description: About 22,000 students in England were told they were given loans by mistake and must immediately pay the money back.

headlines.title: I feared my son had a brain tumour but he'd been poisoned with vitamin D
headlines.description: Investigations found Roo had been accidentally poisoned with a dose of vitamin D prescribed for growing pains.

headlines.title: Satellite images reveal scale of Israeli demolitions as Lebanese villages destroyed
headlines.description: BBC Verify analysis found more than 1,400 buildings had been destroyed since 2 March.

headlines.title: Frank Gardner: What is China's role in the Iran war?
headlines.description: BBC Security Correspondent Frank Gardner explains how the world's second-largest economy fits into the Gulf conflict.

headlines.title: Is the US blockade of Iran working?
headlines.description: BBC Verify has been looking into whether the US blockade of Iran near the Strait of Hormuz is working.

headlines.title: Prehistoric hippo bones a 'once in a lifetime' find in cave under Welsh castle
headlines.description: Archaeologists have so far uncovered "extremely rare" evidence of early humans and animals at the cave.

headlines.title: From friendship to friction: Inside the growing tensions between Trump and Starmer
headlines.description: BBC’s Sarah Smith examines how the Iran war has led to discrepancies between the US president and UK prime minister.

headlines.title: Back to books - Sweden's schools cutting back on digital learning
headlines.description: Swedish classrooms swap laptops for books, pens and paper, raising concerns from the tech sector.

headlines.title: Chris Mason: Ministers have to close asylum loopholes while protecting genuinely vulnerable
headlines.description: There has been a stickiness to the electorate's concerns about issues of immigration and asylum, Chris Mason writes.

headlines.title: Hunt for escaped wolf sparks AI fakes and meme coins
headlines.description: Hundreds have been deployed to find Neukgu, a young wolf that has eluded capture for a week and counting.

headlines.title: Fake damage and imaginary watches - how AI images are being used in insurance scams
headlines.description: An insurer reports a 71% rise in fraudulent claims, driven partly by an increase in faked images.

headlines.title: Big energy shock will push up prices, Bank boss tells BBC
headlines.description: Bank of England governor says the Iran war energy shock makes the next interest rate decision "very, very difficult".

headlines.title: Horse urine perfume: How online bargains may be dangerous
headlines.description: Experts warn of hidden risk of counterfeits, while the government consults on stricter product safety rules.

headlines.title: 'LIV Golf season to continue' amid collapse rumours
headlines.description: LIV Golf chief executive Scott O'Neil reportedly tells players that the 2026 season will continue uninterrupted amid rumours that the tour is on the verge of collapse.

headlines.title: Shoe brand Allbirds' shares rise 580% after it pivots from footwear to AI
headlines.description: The company is selling off its shoe brand as it plans to shift to providing technology infrastructure.

headlines.title: Social media leaders called to Downing Street over children's safety
headlines.description: Top executives from firms such as Meta and YouTube will be asked what they are doing to protect children.

headlines.title: More big energy users to get help as support plan expanded
headlines.description: A scheme to cut bills for firms that are heavy energy users is being extended to cover an additional 3,000 businesses.

headlines.title: BBC News app
headlines.description: Top stories, breaking news, live reporting, and follow news topics that match your interests

headlines.title: Has Trump given up on the midterms?
headlines.description: The president is losing public support over the Iran war.

headlines.title: Can we afford to keep the UK safe?
headlines.description: Former Nato chief warns UK's national security 'in peril'.

headlines.title: An epic gangster biopic of the Kray brothers, starring Tom Hardy
headlines.description: The rise and decline of the Kray twins, London's most legendary gangsters.

headlines.title: LIV Golf to continue 'at full throttle' amid collapse rumours
headlines.description: LIV Golf chief executive Scott O'Neil reportedly tells players that the 2026 season will continue uninterrupted amid rumours that the tour is on the verge of collapse.

headlines.title: Seven goals, fastest strike and two red cards - Bayern-Real delivers drama
headlines.description: The quickest strike of this season's Champions League, seven goals, two red cards and the 15-time winners exiting in anger - once again the knockout stages of Europe's elite tournament delivered drama in spades.

headlines.title: Play-off pending? How draw at Etihad could set up thrilling finale
headlines.description: Man City's meeting against Arsenal may go a long way to deciding the destination of the Premier League trophy in May, but how close a finish could it be?

headlines.title: O'Sullivan to play China's He in Crucible opener - see full draw
headlines.description: Seven-time winner Ronnie O'Sullivan begins his bid for a record-breaking eighth World Snooker Championship title with a match against China's debutant He Guoqiang at the Crucible.

headlines.title: Arsenal limp to semis but Arteta wanted fire - does style matter?
headlines.description: Mikel Arteta wanted "fire" from his Arsenal players but instead got a sluggish performance as the Gunners did what was needed to reach the Champions League semi-finals.

headlines.title: £124m spent but Newcastle no closer to striker solution
headlines.description: Newcastle United spent £124m to replace departing Alexander Isak, but Eddie Howe's men now seem no closer to finding their striking solution.

headlines.title: Bayern strike late to beat Real Madrid in seven-goal thriller
headlines.description: Bayern Munich leave it late in a back-and-forth classic to beat Real Madrid 4-3 to set up a meeting with Paris St-Germain in the semi-finals of the Champions League.
